name: Globalstar, Inc.
business: Globalstar, Inc. provides Mobile Satellite Services (MSS), including voice and data communications services to retail, business and governmental customers as well as wholesale satellite capacity services. The company also offers Commercial IoT products, SPOT devices, Duplex voice/data, and terrestrial spectrum solutions.
sellsTo: Commercial IoT customers (transportation, construction, agriculture, forestry, oil and gas)
namedSuppliers: Macdonald, Dettwiler and Associates Corporation (MDA Space)

exposures.sentence: Through its global satellite network, Globalstar, Inc. provides Mobile Satellite Services (MSS), including voice and data communications services to retail, business and governmental customers as well as wholesale satellite capacity services.

Our constellation of Low Earth Orbit ("LEO") satellites is designed to maximize the probability that at least one satellite is visible from any point on the Earth's surface between the latitudes 70 north and 70 south.

We have an exclusive partnership with Parsons Corporation, a governmental services company, to utilize the Globalstar System to provide an innovative solution design to enhance resilience against disrupted communication pathways.

Prospective spectrum partners, including cable companies, wireless carriers, system integrators, utilities and other infrastructure operators, are able to benefit from access to uniform and increasingly borderless spectrum working across geographies.
We have announced plans to expand our ground network to support the Extended MSS Network with construction underway at critical sites around the globe.
We offer small satellite transmitter modules, such as the STX-3, ST-150 and ST100, and chips, such as our proprietary ASIC, which enable products that integrate our modules to access our network.
Our subscribers use our Commercial IoT devices for a host of applications, including to track assets, such as: cargo containers and rail cars, and to monitor utility meters and oil and gas assets.
We provide Commercial IoT services to customers operating in a variety of industries, primarily government, transportation, construction, agriculture and forestry.

Current customers include various governmental agencies, such as the Federal Emergency Management Agency, U.S. Army, U.S. Air Force, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, U.S. Forest Service and U.K. Ministry of Defence, as well as other organizations, such as BP, Shell and The Salvation Army.
Our networks and those of our third-party service providers and customers may be vulnerable to cyber-attacks and other security breaches, which could have significant negative consequences.

The risk of security breaches is likely to continue to increase due to several factors, including (i) the increasing use of machine learning, artificial intelligence ("AI") and other sophisticated techniques to initiate cyber and phishing attacks...
In February 2025, we entered into another agreement with MDA Space pursuant to which we expect to acquire more than 50 third-generation C-3 System satellites related to the Extended MSS Network.

catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, we released the RM200M two-way module, designed to integrate into IoT and industrial solutions.

catalysts.event: Exercise of right to purchase XCOM intellectual property assets
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we exercised our right under the Intellectual Property License Agreement (the License Agreement) with XCOM Labs, Inc. (now known as Virewirx, Inc.) ( XCOM ) to purchase intellectual property assets relating to the development and commercialization of XCOM s technologies for wireless spectrum innovations, including XCOM RAN systems.

catalysts.event: Delivery of replacement satellites - first set
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect delivery of the 17 replacement satellites during 2026, with the first set expected in early 2026 and the second set in mid-2026.

catalysts.event: Delivery of replacement satellites - second set
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect delivery of the 17 replacement satellites during 2026, with the first set expected in early 2026 and the second set in mid-2026.

catalysts.event: Launch of replacement satellites - first launch
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect to complete both launches during 2026 with the first launch, consisting of eight replacement satellites, anticipated during the first half of 2026, and the launch of the second set of replacement satellites anticipated during the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Launch of replacement satellites - second launch
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect to complete both launches during 2026 with the first launch, consisting of eight replacement satellites, anticipated during the first half of 2026, and the launch of the second set of replacement satellites anticipated during the second half of 2026.
We are currently developing a new two-way SPOT product, which is currently expected to launch in late 2026.

catalysts.event: FCC petition for C-3 System U.S. market access
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we filed a petition with the FCC requesting U.S. market access for the C-3 System.
